item.title: White bird?
item.content:encoded:
According to the journal pages, the white bird seems to be kind of the turning point where things really start going wrong, yet it is never addressed anywhere else in the report. That feels a bit off to me.

Also, I think it could have done without the -C disemboweling themselves and building structures. An SCP doesn't need brutal violence to be good.

I'll settle on a no-vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ehh, some of the ideas are good but the whole cognitohazard and the -C deal seems over the top and kind of clashes with the theme of the rest of the article.

item.title: Imperialist Pig Dogs?
item.content:encoded:
Found an instance of imperial units being used instead of SI.

about 7 miles east of SCP-3066. 75% of the discharged mass is composed of 5 × 10 inch sheets of parchment paper.

Probably just done out of habit, but I'd go change it.

As for the actual thing; it was kinda weird to read. I really don't know how I feel about it. I'll settle on a no-vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This makes me think of a Manly Wade Wellman story as written by Thomas Ligotti.
Thanks for the links to the rest of the arc. There's a lot of horror there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So I downvoted, for a couple reason.

You've got a few instances of unsubstantiated claims (such as being abandoned in a specific year), use terms before they're defined (the Eckhart-anatol discharge), and overall, the story didn't really hold my attention much throughout the skip.

While I appreciate the novel concept, the execution really sinks this for me. Downvote for now.

item.title: Not bad
item.content:encoded: Despite the fact i gave an up-vote this story is still lacking in some aspects such as grammar, and what appears to be laziness, however i don't have the right to call some one out on what i think is being lazy, because i'm guilty of this as well. Besides that this is a good SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nearby is a circular borehole with a 3.3-meter diameter.

Wording seems a little awkward. First thought I had was "a borehole with a 3.3m diameter what?"

Maybe "with a diameter of 3.3 meters"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The discharge events only happen at night, so the event was triggered by the MTF members, then happened later, after sunset. I suppose I could make that a lot clearer.

Thanks for the compliments!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You have my upvote for the line about extraterrestrial activity. When aliens are a better explanation than what's actually going on, shit's wrong.

The discharge events were creative. The journal is a pretty decent "descent into madness" kind of thing, and isn't overly explainy. The exploration log, though, left me a tad confused, so just to check: entering the archways will cause a person to disappear and a discharge event to happen. But this doesn't happen immediately, and it's possible for discharge events to happen on their own. Somehow, as I was reading, I got the idea that discharge was only caused by people going in the archways, and it kind of sounds like the effect should be immediate from the description.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I came up this because I was unhappy.

Thanks to DJMaster, Salvagebar, and Qwag for the feedback!

Image credit is here. The image is under Creative Commons, and was slightly edited by me.

Check out the rest of the Linville Arc!

SCP-3025: Lucidity

SCP-3058: Concavity

SCP-3076: Avidity
